title: Schistocarpha eupatorioides
taxonomy: Schistocarpha eupatorioides is classified as a genus within the flowering plant family Asteraceae. This family is commonly known as the daisy or sunflower family. Schistocarpha eupatorioides is native to Central and South America, and its classification is further divided into various species within the genus. The species within the Schistocarpha eupatorioides genus are typically characterized by their distinctive leaf shape and flower structures. Further classification may also include the specific species within this genus.

intro: Schistocarpha eupatorioides, also known as the "Eupatorium-leaved plant", is a species of flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae). The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments. The bioactive constituents of Schistocarpha eupatorioides include triterpenoid saponins, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are thought to contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is commonly used in herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are used to support immune function and alleviate symptoms associated with infections and inflammatory conditions.
